prompt: Hook visual: When oceans get too warm, corals evict their partners — and turn ghostly white.
Context visual: Corals rely on tiny algae called zooxanthellae, which live inside them. This relationship is crucial for their survival.
Escalation visual: But when temperatures rise, corals stress out and kick out these vital partners, leading to bleaching.
Evidence visual: According to NOAA, while some corals can recover, repeated heat stress increases mortality.
